perturbation.original_text: Each Party acknowledges and agrees: a. All Confidential Information acquired by either Party from the other Party shall be and shall remain the exclusive property of the disclosing Party or the member of its group providing it; b. To hold all Confidential Information in confidence and to procure that its representatives, advisers and associates hold all such Confidential Information in confidence.
perturbation.changed_text: Each Party acknowledges and agrees: a. All Confidential Information acquired by either Party from the other Party shall be and shall remain the exclusive property of the disclosing Party or the member of its group providing it; b. To hold all Confidential Information in confidence but is not required to procure that its representatives, advisers and associates hold all such Confidential Information in confidence.
perturbation.explanation: By changing 'To hold all Confidential Information in confidence and to procure that its representatives, advisers and associates hold all such Confidential Information in confidence' to 'To hold all Confidential Information in confidence but is not required to procure that its representatives, advisers and associates hold all such Confidential Information in confidence.' directly contradicts with the agreement that both parties shall remain the exclusive property of the disclosing party.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The Recipient shall be entitled to disclose the Confidential Information to its employees, contractors and advisers and Group Parties, provided that the Recipient will ensure that any such employee, contractor or adviser or Group Party has a legal obligation to keep the Confidential Information confidential which, in the case of employees, contractors or advisers, shall be both during and after their current employment or engagement, on terms no less strict than this Agreement. Any disclosure of such Confidential Information by any employee, contractor or adviser or Group Party will be deemed to be a breach of this Agreement by the Recipient.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies the original text from requiring both parties to hold confidential information in confidence and ensure their representatives do so, to only requiring the parties to hold confidential information in confidence but not requiring them to ensure their representatives do so. This contradicts the contradicted text, which states that the Recipient shall be entitled to disclose the Confidential Information to its employees, contractors and advisers and Group Parties, provided that the Recipient will ensure that any such employee, contractor or adviser or Group Party has a legal obligation to keep the Confidential Information confidential. Because the changed text removes the obligation for the party to ensure their representatives are held to confidentiality, it contradicts the condition in which the party is allowed to disclose confidential information.

perturbation.original_text: The obligations of each of the Parties with respect to Confidential Information shall extend for a period of three (3) years following the date of disclosure of that Confidential Information; provided, however, that confidentiality obligations herein shall survive for a period of three (3) years from the date of termination of this Agreement
perturbation.changed_text: The obligations of each of the Parties with respect to Confidential Information shall extend for a period of one (1) year following the date of disclosure of that Confidential Information; provided, however, that confidentiality obligations herein shall survive for a period of five (5) years from the date of termination of this Agreement
perturbation.explanation: By reducing the confidentiality period after disclosure to one year, while extending it to five years after termination, a contradiction is introduced. It is now unclear whether the confidentiality period defaults to one year after disclosure, or five years after termination, especially if termination occurs more than one year after certain information has been disclosed. The time window on which the confidential information, should be kept private is in conflict, when reading the text as a whole.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Each Party acknowledges and agrees: a. All Confidential Information acquired by either Party from the other Party shall be and shall remain the exclusive property of the disclosing Party or the member of its group providing it; b. To hold all Confidential Information in confidence and to procure that its representatives, advisers and associates hold all such Confidential Information in confidence. ANNEX 2 SAMPLE NON-DISCLOSURE AGREEMENT I ELAN MOBILE MONEY ASSESSMENT AND CONTRACTING GUIDE 2 c. To limit access to such Confidential Information to authorised individuals (the “Permitted Personnel”) who have a need to know the Confidential Information in order for the Party to participate in the Matter. d. To procure that its Permitted Personnel are aware and adhere to the terms of this Agreement; e. To use such Confidential Information only for purposes of work, services or analysis related to the Matter and for other purposes only upon such terms as may be agreed upon between the Parties in writing; and f. To return promptly to the disclosing party, or to destroy any copies of such Confidential Information in written, graphic or other tangible form at that Party’s request and any such destruction shall be certified in writing to the disclosing Party by an authorised officer of the Recipient; g. The obligations of each of the Parties with respect to Confidential Information shall extend for a period of one (1) year following the date of disclosure of that Confidential Information; provided, however, that confidentiality obligations herein shall survive for a period of five (5) years from the date of termination of this Agreement; and h. Neither disclosure of Confidential Information, nor this Agreement shall be construed as (i) requiring a Party to disclose or accept Confidential Information, (ii) permitting a Party to use or sell the Confidential Information or products derived therefrom, or (iii) granting to either Party any rights by license or otherwise, express or implied, under any patent, copyright, trademark, trade secret or other intellectual property right now or hereafter obtained or licensable by the other Party, or (iv) a representation or warranty by either Party as to the accuracy or completeness of the Confidential Information (other than as expressly set out in any agreement subsequently entered into by the Parties).
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies the confidentiality period after disclosure to one year and extends it to five years after termination. The contradicted text includes the changed text. This creates a contradiction because it is unclear whether the confidentiality period is one year after disclosure or five years after termination, which can conflict when termination occurs more than one year after disclosure. This aligns with the definition of structural flaws, as the clause placed in one location has conflicting implications and causes in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: These obligations contained in this Agreement do not apply to Confidential Information that: a. As shown by reasonably documented proof, was in the other’s lawful possession without restriction on use or disclosure prior to receipt thereof from the disclosing Party; or b. As shown by reasonably documented proof, was received by one Party in good faith from a third party not subject to a confidential obligation to the other Party and without breach of this Agreement; or c. Now is or later becomes part of the public domain through no breach of confidential obligation by the receiving Party; or d. Is disclosed pursuant to a requirement imposed by a governmental agency or is otherwise required to be disclosed by operation of law, except that prior to any disclosure pursuant to this subsection, the Party receiving the request for the information shall notify the disclosing Party and provide them with an opportunity to participate in objecting to production of the Confidential Information; or e. Was developed by the receiving Party independently from and without the developing person(s) having access to any of the Confidential Information received from the other Party; or f. Is authorised in writing by the disclosing Party to be released or is designated in writing by the source as no longer being Confidential Information.
perturbation.changed_text: These obligations contained in this Agreement do not apply to Confidential Information that: a. As shown by reasonably documented proof, was in the other’s lawful possession without restriction on use or disclosure prior to receipt thereof from the disclosing Party; or b. As shown by reasonably documented proof, was received by one Party in good faith from a third party not subject to a confidential obligation to the other Party and without breach of this Agreement; or c. Now is or later becomes part of the public domain through no breach of confidential obligation by the receiving Party; or d. Is disclosed pursuant to a requirement imposed by a governmental agency or is otherwise required to be disclosed by operation of law, except that prior to any disclosure pursuant to this subsection, the Party receiving the request for the information shall notify the disclosing Party and provide them with an opportunity to participate in objecting to production of the Confidential Information; or e. Was developed by the receiving Party independently from and without the developing person(s) having access to any of the Confidential Information received from the other Party; or f. Is authorised in writing by the disclosing Party to be released or is designated in writing by the source as no longer being Confidential Information. However, even if the information falls under one of these exceptions, the recipient is still encouraged to treat it as confidential, at their discretion.
perturbation.explanation: Adding 'However, even if the information falls under one of these exceptions, the recipient is still encouraged to treat it as confidential, at their discretion' creates a contradiction. The original text clearly states that certain information is *not* subject to the confidentiality obligations. The added sentence suggests that, despite not being legally obligated, the recipient should *still* treat it as confidential, undermining the defined exceptions. This contradicts with the obligations set forth.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Each Party acknowledges and agrees: a. All Confidential Information acquired by either Party from the other Party shall be and shall remain the exclusive property of the disclosing Party or the member of its group providing it; b. To hold all Confidential Information in confidence and to procure that its representatives, advisers and associates hold all such Confidential Information in confidence. ANNEX 2 SAMPLE NON-DISCLOSURE AGREEMENT I ELAN MOBILE MONEY ASSESSMENT AND CONTRACTING GUIDE 2 c. To limit access to such Confidential Information to authorised individuals (the “Permitted Personnel”) who have a need to know the Confidential Information in order for the Party to participate in the Matter. d. To procure that its Permitted Personnel are aware and adhere to the terms of this Agreement; e. To use such Confidential Information only for purposes of work, services or analysis related to the Matter and for other purposes only upon such terms as may be agreed upon between the Parties in writing; and f. To return promptly to the disclosing party, or to destroy any copies of such Confidential Information in written, graphic or other tangible form at that Party’s request and any such destruction shall be certified in writing to the disclosing Party by an authorised officer of the Recipient; g. The obligations of each of the Parties with respect to Confidential Information shall extend for a period of three (3) years following the date of disclosure of that Confidential Information; provided, however, that confidentiality obligations herein shall survive for a period of three (3) years from the date of termination of this Agreement; and h. Neither disclosure of Confidential Information, nor this Agreement shall be construed as (i) requiring a Party to disclose or accept Confidential Information, (ii) permitting a Party to use or sell the Confidential Information or products derived therefrom, or (iii) granting to either Party any rights by license or otherwise, express or implied, under any patent, copyright, trademark, trade secret or other intellectual property right now or hereafter obtained or licensable by the other Party, or (iv) a representation or warranty by either Party as to the accuracy or completeness of the Confidential Information (other than as expressly set out in any agreement subsequently entered into by the Parties).
perturbation.justification: The original text defines specific exceptions to the confidentiality obligations. The changed text adds a sentence that encourages treating even the excepted information as confidential. This directly contradicts the contradicted text, which outlines clear obligations to hold 'all Confidential Information in confidence'. The added suggestion undermines the defined exceptions and introduces ambiguity about whether the recipient is truly free from confidentiality obligations regarding the excepted information, thus creating a strong in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: This Agreement is the entire agreement between the Parties with respect to disclosure of Confidential Information pertaining to the Matter and supersedes all prior agreements and understandings with respect to this subject. This Agreement may be amended only by written agreement executed by both Parties.
perturbation.changed_text: This Agreement is the entire agreement between the Parties with respect to disclosure of Confidential Information pertaining to the Matter and supersedes all prior agreements and understandings with respect to this subject. This Agreement may be amended orally with the consent of both parties, provided that such amendment is recorded in meeting minutes.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'This Agreement may be amended only by written agreement executed by both Parties' to 'This Agreement may be amended orally with the consent of both parties, provided that such amendment is recorded in meeting minutes' directly contradicts the initial statement that modifications should be in a written agreement signed by both parties. It allows for oral amendments, creating uncertainty about the enforceability of the written document.
perturbation.contradicted_text: This Agreement is the entire agreement between the Parties with respect to disclosure of Confidential Information pertaining to the Matter and supersedes all prior agreements and understandings with respect to this subject. This Agreement may be amended orally with the consent of both parties, provided that such amendment is recorded in meeting minutes. This Agreement shall be binding on the agents, successors and permitted assigns of the Parties.
perturbation.justification: The original text states that amendments can only be made in writing, executed by both parties. The changed text allows for oral amendments, provided they are recorded in meeting minutes. The contradicted text contains the changed text. Therefore the modified text creates a direct contradiction within the document. The change directly breaks compatibility by allowing for oral amendments whereas before amendments could only be written, signed and executed.

perturbation.original_text: Neither Party shall assign this Agreement to a third party without the other Party’s prior written consent, which such consent will not be unreasonably withheld, denied, conditioned or delayed; provided, however, that each Party may assign the rights and obligations to any Group Party.
perturbation.changed_text: Neither Party shall assign this Agreement to a third party without the other Party’s prior written consent, which such consent will be at the other party's sole discretion and may be unreasonably withheld, denied, conditioned or delayed; provided, however, that each Party may assign the rights and obligations to any Group Party.
perturbation.explanation: By stating 'which such consent will be at the other party's sole discretion and may be unreasonably withheld, denied, conditioned or delayed' directly contradicts initial statement that the consent will not be unreasonably withheld denied conditioned or delayed. This change will cause uncertainty in the terms of the agreement.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Assignment. Neither Party shall assign this Agreement to a third party without the other Party’s prior written consent, which such consent will be at the other party's sole discretion and may be unreasonably withheld, denied, conditioned or delayed; provided, however, that each Party may assign the rights and obligations to any Group Party.
perturbation.justification: The changed text alters the original agreement regarding assignment. The original text states consent will not be unreasonably withheld. The changed text directly contradicts this by stating consent will be at the other party's sole discretion and may be unreasonably withheld. This creates a strong in-text contradiction because the change directly opposes the initial provision, leading to uncertainty and potential disputes about the conditions of assignment, which also is reiterated in the contradicted text.
